title: Joint Learning of Experiential Rules and Policies for Large Language Model Agents
abstract: arXiv:2606.27136v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: For LLM agents in multi-step interactive environments, a key challenge is to make effective use of accumulated interaction experience. Existing work has typically separated two uses of such experience: keeping it outside the model as natural-language rules for later prompting, or using trajectories and feedback to update the model parameters. The former is easy to interpret but can fall out of sync with the evolving policy; the latter improves the policy more broadly but provides only limited correction for local mistakes in sparse-reward settings. We present Joint Learning of Experiential Rules and Policies for LLM Agents (JERP), which updates a long-term experiential-rule pool and the policy from the same interaction trajectories. At decision time, JERP retrieves task-relevant rules and conditions the agent on them together with the interaction history. After each episode, it uses the collected trajectories both to optimize the policy

Title: Joint Learning of Experiential Rules and Policies for Large Language Model Agents
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27136v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: For LLM agents in multi-step interactive environments, a key challenge is to make effective use of accumulated interaction experience. Existing work has typically separated two uses of such experience: keeping it outside the model as natural-language rules for later prompting, or using trajectories and feedback to update the model parameters. The former is easy to interpret but can fall out of sync with the evolving policy; the latter improves the policy more broadly but provides only limited correction for local mistakes in sparse-reward settings. We present Joint Learning of Experiential Rules and Policies for LLM Agents (JERP), which updates a long-term experiential-rule pool and the policy from the same interaction trajectories. At decision time, JERP retrieves task-relevant rules and conditions the agent on them together with the interaction history. After each episode, it uses the collected trajectories both to optimize the policy
translated_title: 大型语言模型代理的经验规则与策略联合学习
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.27136v1 公告类型：新文 摘要：在多步交互环境中，大型语言模型代理面临的一个关键挑战是有效利用积累的交互经验。现有工作通常将此类经验的两种用途分离：将其作为自然语言规则保留在模型外部以供后续提示使用，或使用轨迹和反馈来更新模型参数。前者易于解释，但可能随策略演变而失去同步；后者能更广泛地改进策略，但在稀疏奖励设置中对局部错误的纠正有限。我们提出了大型语言模型代理的经验规则与策略联合学习（JERP），该方法从相同的交互轨迹中更新长期经验规则库和策略。在决策时，JERP检索任务相关规则，并将其与交互历史一起作为代理的条件。每轮结束后，它使用收集的轨迹来优化策略。
tldr: JERP通过从相同交互轨迹中联合更新经验规则库和策略，解决了LLM代理在稀疏奖励环境中策略与规则不同步及局部错误纠正有限的问题。
en_tdlr: JERP addresses the issues of policy-rule desynchronization and limited local error correction in sparse-reward settings for LLM agents by jointly updating the experiential rule pool and policy from the same interaction trajectories.
